name: South Carolina (United States)

colors.symbolism: the color of the colonial uniforms of the South Carolina militia during the Revolutionary War
the silver crescent worn on the front of the militiamen's caps

description: An indigo-blue field with a white sabal palmetto tree in the center and a white crescent in the upper-hoist quarter.
history: South Carolina's flag descends from the banner devised in 1775 by Colonel William Moultrie at the request of the Revolutionary Council of Safety: a blue field bearing a white crescent, after the silver crescent on the militiamen's caps. Moultrie's flag flew at Fort Sullivan during its successful defense against the British fleet on 28 June 1776 — a victory credited to the spongy palmetto logs of the fort's walls. The palmetto was added to the design in January 1861 to commemorate the battle, producing the current flag.

features.symbolism: the indigo blue of the uniforms of the South Carolina militia during the Revolutionary War

features.role: a white crescent in the upper-hoist quarter, points opening to the upper fly
features.symbolism: represents the silver gorget or cap-badge worn by the troops of Colonel William Moultrie at Fort Sullivan in June 1776

features.role: a white sabal palmetto tree at the center
features.symbolism: the palmetto logs of Fort Sullivan absorbed British cannonballs during the 28 June 1776 battle, allowing the colonial defenders to repel the Royal Navy
